## Steps to Reproduce
Proccess this code with `ktlint --format`:

```
package com.my.lint.rules

import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule
import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode

class BreakKtlintFormatRule : Rule(
    RuleId("my-ruleset:my-rule-id"),
    Rule.About(maintainer = "Me", repositoryUrl = "repositoryUrl", issueTrackerUrl = "issueTrackerUrl")
) {

    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
        node: ASTNode,
        autoCorrect: Boolean,
        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit
    ) {

        if (node.text.contains("this")
            || node.text.contains("that")
        ) {
            emit(node.startOffset, "My violation detail", false)
        }
    }
}
```

* Name and version (or code for custom task) of integration used (Gradle plugin, Maven plugin, command line, custom Gradle task): Command line
* Version of Gradle used (if applicable): N/A
* Operating System and version: macOS 13.2

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/IndentationRule.kt`

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

content.text:
The issue was caused by a `NullPointerException` in `IndentationRule.isElvisOperator` when an `OPERATION_REFERENCE` node had no child nodes. This occurred because the code was directly accessing `firstChildNode.elementType` without checking if `firstChildNode` was null. Although it is unusual for an `OPERATION_REFERENCE` to have no children in a valid Kotlin AST, the parser or other rules might occasionally produce such nodes, or they might occur during intermediate processing.

I have fixed this by using the safe-call operator (`?.`) when accessing `elementType` on `firstChildNode`. I also applied the same fix to several other locations in the codebase where similar unsafe accesses were found, ensuring better robustness across multiple rules.

Specifically, I updated the following files:
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/IndentationRule.kt`: Updated `isElvisOperator` to use `firstChildNode?.elementType`.
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/MultilineExpressionWrapping.kt`: Updated `isElvisOperator` to use `firstChildNode?.elementType`.
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/MultiLineIfElseRule.kt`: Added a null check for `firstChildNode` when checking for an `IF` element type.
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/WrappingRule.kt`: Added a null check for `firstChildNode` when checking for a `DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION`.

These changes resolve the reported crash and prevent similar issues in related rules. All existing tests passed after applying these changes.
